summary: Geographic mislabel in WIN-067 is fixed in two places (curmudgeon Option (a) — rhetorically safer): (1) detail_extra paragraph (ii) substitutes 'The observed Antarctic-interior gravity — the Antarctic-latitude gravity value cited in the objection (~9.826 m/s²) —' with text that drops 'interior', anchors 9.826 m/s² to ~70°S via the WGS84 Somigliana formula, names this anchoring as a deliberate concession to the dome defender's own latitude reference, and discloses that re-anchoring to true interior (80°S = 9.8306 m/s²) would raise the ratio to ~5,600×; (2) tldr_evidence is rewritten to remove 'At 80°S, measured gravity is 9.826 m/s²' (which carried the same mislabel) and replace it with the same ~70°S framing. ISS-1355 was an orphan (not in any prior expansion entry) — analyst opportunistically batched it into EXP-215 because (a) same WIN, (b) same c6 review cycle, (c) the tldr_evidence rewrite for ISS-1356 already touched the affected sentence, making a separate one-line patch wasteful. EXP-215 Patch 2 (tldr_evidence) and Patch 5 (detail_extra paragraph ii) carry the exact replacement strings.
